After the Death of Procopius Valens constrains those who composed the Synod, and All Christians, to profess Arianism.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: After the Death of Procopius Valens constrains those who composed the Synod, and All Christians, to profess Arianism.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor having thus successfully terminated the conflict, immediately began to move against the Christians, with the design of converting every sect to Arianism.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But he was especially incensed against those who had composed the Synod at Lampsacus, not only on account of their deposition of the Arian bishops, but because they had anathematized the creed published at Ariminum.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: On arriving therefore at Nicomedia in Bithynia, he sent for Eleusius bishop of Cyzicus, who, as I have before said, closely adhered to the opinions of Macedonius.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Therefore the emperor having convened a council of Arian bishops, commanded Eleusius to give his assent to their faith.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: At first he refused to do so, but on being terrified with threats of banishment and confiscation of property, he was intimidated and assented to the Arian belief.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Immediately afterwards, however, he repented; and returning to Cyzicus, bitterly complained in presence of all the people, asserting that his quiescence was due to violence, and not of his own choice.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He then exhorted them to seek another bishop for themselves, since he had been compelled to renounce his own opinion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the inhabitants of Cyzicus loved and venerated him too much to think of losing him; they therefore refused to be subject to any other bishop, nor would they permit him to retire from his own church: and thus continuing under his oversight, they remained steadfast in their own heresy.
